preview_text:
The model rates Saudi Arabia as the slight favorite with a 40% win probability, while Cape Verde's chances stand at 29%. The margin separating the two sides is modest, positioning this as a competitive fixture rather than a heavily lopsided contest.

The forecast suggests a draw ranks as a plausible outcome at 31%, nearly as likely as a Saudi Arabia victory. The win probabilities cluster within a narrow band—no outcome commands overwhelming confidence—which indicates the match carries genuine uncertainty across all three result types.

The model and market diverge noticeably. Sportsbook consensus elevates Cape Verde's win probability to 40%, a gain of 11 percentage points over the model's estimate, while trimming Saudi Arabia to 32%, an 8-point drop. Polymarket prices align with the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is more accurate; the divergence may reflect information, market efficiency assumptions, or different modeling approaches not captured in the statistical forecast alone.
